_meta.description: Master registry for all VGR books. Single source of truth for production tracking, catalog display, and transmittal. Replaces catalog_meta.json.

stage_rules.option: Loose idea or possibility not yet committed to. Tracked here for awareness only. Hidden from public catalog.
stage_rules.alpha: Materials and idea in source project. No formal production artifacts. Hidden from public catalog.
stage_rules.beta: Working title assigned, ISBNs to be allocated, transmittal submitted to editor, continuous deploy set up for online editions. Hidden from public catalog unless edition.display is explicitly true.
stage_rules.production: Manuscript with editor. Active production pipeline. ISBN assigned. Catalog card visible.
stage_rules.published: Live. Buy links active. Bowker updated. Catalog card visible.

edition_tracks.online: Continuous deploy via sync.py -> vgr-books. No ISBN. Separate from print/ebook pipeline.
edition_tracks.ebook: Via Vellum -> distributor. Requires ISBN. Transmittal form governs.
edition_tracks.print: Via Vellum -> distributor. Requires ISBN. Transmittal form governs.

thumbnail_convention.alpha: thumbnail field is null. Resolves to docs/thumbnails/coming-soon.svg. Alpha books are not shown in the public catalog.
thumbnail_convention.beta: docs/thumbnails/{id}-beta.jpg (or .png/.svg) — working mockup or early cover draft. Place by hand when available.
thumbnail_convention.production_no_slug: docs/thumbnails/{id}.jpg (or .png) — final cover copied from source project collateral/.
thumbnail_convention.production_with_slug: Cover lives in docs/{slug}/ (e.g. cover.jpg, cover.png). Resolved automatically by build_catalog.py convention scan.
thumbnail_convention.source_preference: Use purpose-built thumb images from collateral/ (e.g. gpthumb.png, bsethumb.png) when available. Fall back to 75dpi or web-res variants of full cover. Never use 300dpi print-resolution files.

isbn_blocks.note: New block. All future books use this block, assigned in order from lowest free.

The Art of Gig, Volume 1: Foundations

books.title: Foundations
books.series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
books.label: Vol. 1
books.blurb: Thirty-two essays on the basics of surviving and thriving as an independent: getting oriented, bootstrapping a practice, and managing the consulting psyche.

transmittal.note: Published before this system. Historical transmittal not tracked.

books.title: Superstructures
books.series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
books.label: Vol. 2
books.blurb: Thirty-four essays on the wider landscape of free agency: the history and future of the gig economy, creative frames for client work, and the long haul.

transmittal.note: Published before this system. Historical transmittal not tracked.

books.title: The Yakverse Chronicles
books.series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
books.label: Vol. 3
books.blurb: Thirteen absurdist consulting tales — yaks, gigsters, and capricious clients in increasingly surreal engagements.

books.title: vgr: The Twitter Years (2007–22)
books.blurb: 102 chapters: a curated compendium of 396 singles plus 101 threads — the complete Twitter writings of Venkatesh Rao (@vgr).

books.title: Breaking Smart: How Software is Eating the World
books.blurb: 21 essays on the software revolution — how agile, networked approaches are disrupting industries and remaking society. In English, German, and French.

transmittal.note: Published before this system. Historical transmittal not tracked.

books.title: Mediocratopia
books.blurb: 13 essays on mediocrity as aspiration — optimization resistance, withheld reserves, and the courage to slouch toward a mediocracy utopia.

books.title: The Gervais Principle
books.blurb: The definitive Ribbonfarm series on organizational life through the lens of The Office — sociopaths, clueless middle management, and losers navigating institutional reality.

books.title: Be Slightly Evil
books.blurb: A Machiavellian guide to organizational life — essays on power, leverage, and strategic imperfection for those who live and work in institutions.

books.title: Indexicality
books.subtitle: The Clockless Clock
books.series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
books.label: Vol. 2
books.blurb: Nine chapters on how we anchor ourselves in time without clocks, drawn from the Contraptions serialization.

books.title: Divergence
books.series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
books.label: Vol. 3
books.blurb: On temporal divergence — how timelines branch, histories fork, and alternative presents accumulate.

books.title: Liveness
books.series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
books.label: Vol. 4
books.blurb: On liveness — the phenomenology of the present tense and what it means to be in the live moment.

books.title: The Great Weirding
books.blurb: Essays on the chaotic transformation of the 2016–2020 era — the historical, cultural, and political weirdness that defines our present.

books.title: On Lore
books.blurb: Essays on lore, world-building, and the informal knowledge systems that hold communities together.

books.title: Mediocre Computing
books.blurb: Essays on computing as a creative and intellectual practice for the rest of us — below the frontier, above the mundane.

books.title: Breaking Smart Newsletter (2015–2019)
books.blurb: Newsletter archives from the Breaking Smart era — essays on software eating the world, collected from the active newsletter years.

books.title: Guerrilla Guide to Enterprise 2.0
books.blurb: From the defunct Enterprise 2.0 blog — a practitioner’s guide to social software in organizations.
